result:
**Goal: Add a Plate Tectonics & Mantle Convection simulation mode.**

A 2D cross-section/map view of Earth's lithosphere and asthenosphere with: mantle convection cells (Rayleigh-Bénard style thermal plumes driving plate motion), rigid tectonic plates that drift, collide, and subduct, divergent boundaries with mid-ocean ridge spreading and new crust formation, convergent boundaries with subduction zones generating volcanic arcs and deep ocean trenches, continental collision producing mountain uplift (orogenesis), transform faults with earthquake stress accumulation and sudden rupture events, hotspot volcanism from deep mantle plumes (Hawaiian chain analog), oceanic crust aging/cooling/densifying as it moves from ridges, and a Wilson Cycle of supercontinent assembly and breakup over geological time. Three views: tectonic map with plate boundaries/volcanoes/quake epicenters, mantle convection cross-section with temperature/flow vectors, and 10-metric sparkline time series (plate velocity, volcanic activity, seismic energy, ocean/continental crust ratio, etc.). Six presets: Stable Craton, Mid-Ocean Ridge Spreading, Subduction Zone Cascade, Continental Collision Himalayas, Supercontinent Breakup Pangaea, and Yellowstone Hotspot Plume.

This fills the conspicuous Earth/geological sciences gap, pairs naturally with the existing Planetary Atmosphere mode, and offers spectacular emergent visuals — drifting continents, erupting volcanoes, and earthquake swarms all rendered in ASCII.
